context: Tests whether the agent implements the dunning engine with correct daily-cron design, invoice status filtering, step selection logic, hold_account behavior, email urgency levels, billing_email preference, and the correct default sequence configuration.

checklist.name: Daily cron design
checklist.description: The dunning processor is described or annotated as running daily (e.g. via cron), not triggered per-invoice or per-event

checklist.name: Invoice status filter
checklist.description: The query filtering overdue invoices includes exactly these status values: 'sent', 'viewed', 'partially_paid', 'overdue' (not just 'overdue', and not including 'draft' or 'paid')

checklist.name: void_at filter
checklist.description: The overdue invoice query also excludes invoices where void_at is not null

checklist.name: Step selection logic
checklist.description: Step selection finds the first step where step.days_overdue <= daysOverdue AND step.step_number > lastStepSent (not just the step whose days_overdue matches exactly)

checklist.name: hold_account sets credit_hold_reason
checklist.description: The hold_account action sets credit_hold=true AND sets credit_hold_reason that references the invoice number and number of days overdue

checklist.name: Email urgency levels
checklist.description: Dunning email data includes an urgency field with three tiers: 'medium' (up to 30 days), 'high' (31-60 days), 'critical' (over 60 days)

checklist.name: billing_email preference
checklist.description: Dunning emails are sent to billing_email when available, falling back to the general email address

checklist.name: Payment link included
checklist.description: Dunning emails include a payment_link or payment URL so the customer can pay in a single click

checklist.name: Default sequence step count
checklist.description: The default dunning_config.json contains exactly 5 steps

checklist.name: Default sequence timing
checklist.description: The 5 default steps trigger at: 1 day, 7 days, 14 days, 30 days, and 60 days overdue

checklist.name: hold_account at 30 days
checklist.description: The hold_account action is at step 4 / 30 days overdue (not at 90+ days)

checklist.name: Tiered customer handling
checklist.description: The notes or code describe treating high-value accounts differently — account manager outreach rather than just automated emails
